title: North Island vs South Island for Families — Which to Choose First?
meta_desc: North vs South Island for families: which to choose first? Discover activity density, drive times & cost difference
intro: When planning a family trip to New Zealand, the biggest dilemma is often which island to visit first. With the North Island offering a plethora of cultural experiences and the South Island boasting breathtaking landscapes, it's essential to consider factors like activity density, drive times, and cost. For instance, a family of four can expect to pay around $200-$300 NZD per night for a decent motel in Auckland, compared to $250-$350 NZD in Queenstown.

sections.heading: Activity Density: North Island vs South Island
sections.body: The North Island is home to a wide range of activities, including visiting the famous Waitomo Glowworm Caves ($50-$70 NZD per person), exploring the geothermal wonders of Rotorua, and learning about Maori culture in Auckland. In contrast, the South Island offers a more laid-back atmosphere, with popular activities like hiking in Abel Tasman National Park ($15-$30 NZD per person) and taking a scenic drive along the Milford Road.

sections.heading: Drive Times and Logistics
list: Wellington to Picton: 3.5 hours plus ferry ride

sections.heading: Age Considerations: Which Island is Best for Your Family?
sections.body: Families with younger children may prefer the North Island, where many attractions cater specifically to kids, such as the Auckland Zoo ($25-$35 NZD per person) and the Wellington Zoo ($20-$30 NZD per person). On the other hand, families with older children and teenagers may enjoy the more adventurous activities offered in the South Island, like skiing in Queenstown ($100-$150 NZD per person per day) or hiking in the Southern Alps.

sections.heading: Cost Comparison: North Island vs South Island

rows: North Island: $50-$100 NZD per person per day
South Island: $70-$150 NZD per person per day

sections.heading: What You Miss if You Only Visit One Island
sections.body: If you only visit the North Island, you'll miss out on the stunning natural beauty of the South Island, including the picturesque town of Wanaka and the breathtaking scenery of Milford Sound. On the other hand, if you only visit the South Island, you'll miss out on the rich cultural heritage of the North Island, including the Auckland Museum ($20-$30 NZD per person) and the Te Papa Museum in Wellington (free admission).
